analysis_metadata.specification: Matter Core Specification 1.5, Section 5.9 Network Recovery

property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: FSM enforces CASE session establishment before credential provisioning through mandatory state transitions
FSM abstraction level doesn't model individual message operations or encryption checks
FSM enforces user consent check at AwaitingAdministrator state before allowing recovery connection establishment
FSM transitions to RecoveryAborted when consent not granted, with no path to CredentialsProvisioned from there
FSM explicitly checks received_over_operational_network in guard and has rejection transition for violations
FSM initializes reconnect_timer to 120 and only allows transition to RecoveryAnnouncing when timer == 0
FSM retrieves RecoveryIdentifier and passes it to advertise_recovery_payload function
FSM starts after credential change; doesn't model Administrator's preparatory actions
FSM explicitly suspends autonomous reconnection in CASESessionEstablished transition with state invariant enforcement
All transitions to RecoveryAborted explicitly resume autonomous reconnection
FSM models automatic transition from CASESessionEstablished to NodeFailSafeArmed with 60s timeout
FSM guard enforces 60s window, transitions to RecoveryAborted if exceeded
FSM handles timeout case but doesn't explicitly model command failure scenario separate from timeout
FSM explicitly models credential provisioning transitions with appropriate commands
FSM guard enforces has_new_credentials == true before ConnectNetwork transition
FSM explicitly passes new_operational_credentials to connection function
FSM explicitly terminates recovery connection with state invariant enforcement
FSM guard checks both operational network and CASE session for CommissioningComplete
FSM requires OperationalDiscovered state before CommissioningComplete can be accepted
FSM models announcement timer and Extended Announcement, assuming function implementations are correct
FSM does not model reconnection attempts in AwaitingAdministrator and RecoveryConnectionEstablished states despite being in recovery mode
FSM explicitly models transition from ExtendedAnnouncementEnded back to RecoveryAnnouncing
FSM doesn't model Administrator's internal state or preconditions
FSM doesn't model external network state
FSM doesn't model individual transaction types or access control at transaction level
FSM function descriptions mention OpCode for distinguishing recovery from commissioning
FSM models CASE session establishment transition over recovery connection
FSM doesn't model transaction type restrictions or usage policies
FSM retrieves error codes and includes in advertisement payload
FSM models continued reconnection attempts in ExtendedAnnouncementEnded state
Uniqueness is a system-wide property not verifiable at FSM level
FSM states are mutually exclusive by definition with contradictory recovery_mode invariants
FSM explicitly terminates recovery connection with state invariant enforcement
FSM function descriptions mention OpCode identification mechanism
FSM structure enforces state ordering through transition guards and prerequisites
FSM models both activities in RecoveryAnnouncing stay transition, though not in all recovery states
FSM shows old and new credentials coexisting during recovery; atomic replacement only at final NormalOperation transition
FSM action ordering enforces remove before add in combined transition
Human decision-making and node identification not modeled in FSM
FSM has no timeout transition from AdminFailSafeArmed state; Administrator can abandon recovery indefinitely after arming fail-safe
FSM function descriptions confirm RecoveryID inclusion in both BLE and WiFi discovery
Detection mechanism is implementation-specific, not modeled in FSM

attack_path.violation: Reconnection attempts stop - no stay transition with attempt_reconnect in AwaitingAdministrator
Still no reconnection attempts - no stay transition in RecoveryConnectionEstablished
Reconnection suspended here, but should have continued until this point

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: FSM only models reconnection attempts in RecoveryAnnouncing and ExtendedAnnouncementEnded states via stay transitions. When node transitions to AwaitingAdministrator or RecoveryConnectionEstablished, there are no stay transitions with reconnection attempts, creating gap period where spec-required continuous reconnection stops.

specification_evidence.quote: Recovery Nodes SHALL attempt to reconnect to the existing operational network while in the recovery state.
specification_evidence.context: Discussing behavior during waiting period and recovery announcement. 'Recovery state' includes all states from entering recovery mode until either autonomous reconnection succeeds, recovery completes, or recovery is aborted.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Spec requires continuous reconnection attempts throughout recovery state

specification_evidence.quote: once the secure connection is established, any ongoing attempts taken autonomously by the Recovery Node to re-establish connectivity to the previously configured operational network SHALL be suspended and SHALL NOT interfere with the session established in this step.
specification_evidence.context: Describes suspension point for autonomous reconnection - occurs when CASE session established, not before.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Reconnection should be ONGOING until CASE session established, confirming they should continue in AwaitingAdministrator and RecoveryConnectionEstablished

specification_evidence.quote: Autonomous attempts to re-establish network connectivity SHALL resume in the event of failure of the network recovery procedure.
specification_evidence.context: Confirms autonomous reconnection is expected behavior that should be suspended only temporarily during recovery procedure.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Reconnection is fundamental feature that should continue until explicitly suspended

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Reconnection attempts SHALL continue throughout recovery state until CASE session established
specification_gap.what_fsm_fails_to_model: FSM does not include stay transitions with reconnection actions in AwaitingAdministrator and RecoveryConnectionEstablished states. Only RecoveryAnnouncing and ExtendedAnnouncementEnded have stay transitions modeling autonomous reconnection.
specification_gap.consequence: Gap period exists where node is in recovery mode but not attempting autonomous reconnection. If network becomes available during this window (Administrator has been notified but hasn't completed CASE session yet), node will not autonomously recover despite spec requirement.
specification_gap.security_impact: Forces unnecessary dependency on Administrator-assisted recovery even when autonomous recovery would succeed. Increases recovery time and reduces fault tolerance. Node remains disconnected longer than necessary.

steps: 1. Node enters RecoveryAnnouncing state, starts advertising recovery with concurrent reconnection attempts
2. Network credentials restored (admin cancels change or network becomes available) 10 seconds later
3. Meanwhile: Administrator receives recovery announcement and begins preparing to connect
4. Node transitions: RecoveryAnnouncing -> AwaitingAdministrator
5. Network is NOW available with old credentials working
6. Node in AwaitingAdministrator state but NO LONGER attempting reconnection (FSM has no stay transition for this)
7. Node could autonomously reconnect using old credentials but doesn't try
8. Administrator proceeds with full recovery unnecessarily
9. Impact: Wastes time, exposes RecoveryIdentifier unnecessarily, uses Administrator resources when autonomous recovery would have succeeded
counterexample_scenario.note: This is a design gap where FSM doesn't fully implement spec's continuous reconnection requirement across all recovery states

recommendations: Add stay transition in AwaitingAdministrator state: AwaitingAdministrator -> AwaitingAdministrator with trigger 'reconnect_attempt_tick' and action 'attempt_reconnect(old_credentials)'

Add stay transition in RecoveryConnectionEstablished state: RecoveryConnectionEstablished -> RecoveryConnectionEstablished with trigger 'reconnect_attempt_tick' and action 'attempt_reconnect(old_credentials)'
Ensure suspend_autonomous_reconnect() is only called upon CASE session establishment, not before

attack_path.violation: No timeout transition exists from AdminFailSafeArmed. Node remains in this state indefinitely with no automatic recovery or rollback.

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: FSM models fail-safe timeout only from NodeFailSafeArmed state (before Administrator arms fail-safe). Once Administrator successfully arms fail-safe and transitions to AdminFailSafeArmed, there is NO fail-safe timeout transition. If Administrator then abandons recovery (doesn't send credential provisioning commands), node is stuck indefinitely in AdminFailSafeArmed state with no rollback mechanism.

specification_evidence.quote: Upon successful establishment of the CASE session, the Recovery Node SHALL autonomously arm the fail-safe timer for a timeout of 60 seconds. This is to guard against the Administrator not proceeding with the rest of the flow in a timely fashion, and is analogous to step 6 of the standard commissioning flow.
specification_evidence.context: Describes purpose of fail-safe: guard against Administrator not proceeding. This applies to ALL subsequent steps, not just the Administrator arming fail-safe.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Fail-safe should protect against Administrator abandonment at ANY point after CASE session, including after Administrator arms fail-safe

specification_evidence.quote: Within 60 seconds of establishing the CASE session, the Administrator SHALL arm the fail-safe timer. If the fail-safe could not be successfully armed, the Administrator SHALL terminate the session.
specification_evidence.context: Describes Administrator's responsibility to arm within 60s. But doesn't clarify what happens if Administrator arms successfully then abandons.
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Implies fail-safe continues protecting; Administrator arming is a checkpoint, not an ending

specification_evidence.quote: The Administrator SHALL provide the new credentials of the operational network to the Recovery Node using the AddOrUpdateWiFiNetwork or AddOrUpdateThreadNetwork commands.
specification_evidence.context: Next step after arming fail-safe. If Administrator doesn't do this, what happens?
specification_evidence.gap: Specification doesn't explicitly state fail-safe continues protecting after Administrator arms, but intent from Step 10 is clear: guard against Administrator not proceeding with 'rest of flow'

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Fail-safe shall guard against Administrator not proceeding with rest of flow (Step 10)
specification_gap.what_fsm_fails_to_model: FSM only models fail-safe timeout from NodeFailSafeArmed state. No timeout transition from AdminFailSafeArmed state. No protection if Administrator arms fail-safe successfully but then abandons recovery before provisioning credentials.
specification_gap.consequence: Recovery Node can be locked in AdminFailSafeArmed state indefinitely. No automatic rollback. Node cannot complete recovery, cannot return to autonomous reconnection attempts, and cannot accept new recovery attempts. Requires manual intervention (power cycle, reset) to recover.
specification_gap.security_impact: CRITICAL - Denial of Service vulnerability. Attacker acting as malicious Administrator can: (1) trigger recovery announcement, (2) establish CASE session, (3) arm fail-safe, (4) abandon recovery. Victim node is now locked and unreachable. Legitimate Administrator cannot restart recovery because node is stuck in AdminFailSafeArmed state.

steps: 1. Attacker triggers network credential change to disconnect victim node
2. Victim node enters recovery mode and starts announcing
3. Attacker (as Administrator) receives announcement and establishes connection
4. Attacker establishes CASE session
5. Node autonomously arms fail-safe (60s)
6. Attacker sends ArmFailSafe command within 60s window
7. Transition: NodeFailSafeArmed -> AdminFailSafeArmed
8. Attacker disconnects and abandons recovery
9. Node is stuck in AdminFailSafeArmed state
10. Node cannot timeout (no FSM transition)
11. Node cannot accept new recovery attempts (still in AdminFailSafeArmed)
12. Node remains disconnected indefinitely until manual intervention
Result: Persistent Denial of Service
counterexample_scenario.note: This is an FSM design flaw - fail-safe protection disappears after Administrator successfully arms fail-safe, contrary to spec's intent

recommendations: Add transition: AdminFailSafeArmed -> FailSafeExpired with trigger 'admin_failsafe_timer_expired', guard 'admin_failsafe_timer == 0', actions 'rollback_state(), resume_autonomous_reconnect()'
Add stay transition in AdminFailSafeArmed to decrement admin_failsafe_timer, similar to reconnect_timer decrements  in WaitingReconnect
Clarify in specification: fail-safe timer continues running through ALL recovery steps until CommissioningComplete, not just until Administrator arms fail-safe
Alternative: Make fail-safe a single continuous timer from CASE session establishment through recovery completion, not two separate timers (node 60s, admin separate)

key_findings: FSM is generally well-designed and enforces most security properties correctly
Two clear violations found where FSM doesn't fully implement specification requirements
PROP_021 violation (MEDIUM): Autonomous reconnection doesn't continue in all recovery states as spec requires
PROP_040 violation (CRITICAL): Fail-safe timeout protection missing after Administrator arms fail-safe, enabling DoS attack
Ten properties unverifiable due to FSM abstraction level (message operations, implementation details, external state)
Two properties partially hold due to incomplete modeling (fail-safe arming failure scenarios, credential temporal atomicity)
Most CRITICAL properties (user consent, CASE encryption, operational network enforcement) hold correctly in FSM
recommendations: Add stay transitions with reconnection attempts in AwaitingAdministrator and RecoveryConnectionEstablished states
Add fail-safe timeout transition from AdminFailSafeArmed state to prevent indefinite lock-up
Consider continuous fail-safe timer design rather than two-phase approach
Clarify in specification that fail-safe protects through entire recovery flow, not just initial checkpoints
